_description: Norway and England full 2026 in-tournament logs (group stage x3 + R32 + R16), extracted from ESPN boxscore/roster data. England's group+R32 games and R16 opponent (Mexico) data reused from matches/Mexico_vs_England/; Norway's from matches/Brazil_vs_Norway/. Both R16 games (Norway vs Brazil, England vs Mexico) fetched fresh live from ESPN (events 760504, 760505) since neither folder had the R16 game itself on disk.

elo.source: ml/backtests/nor_eng_qf_point_in_time_elo_replay.py (new script, same methodology as qf_point_in_time_elo_replay.py from Spain vs Belgium)
elo.note: Neither team was in the original 10-team r16_point_in_time_elo_replay.py set; built a fresh replay from data/processed/unified_team_match_panel.csv real scores (results.csv is NA-filled for all WC2026 games, same known staleness bug). Frozen pre-tournament baselines: Norway 1983.84, England 2090.37.

venue.name: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, Florida
venue.note: Neither Norway nor England is USA/MX/CA, so neutral=true per project convention despite US venue. Altitude negligible (sea level), not a factor.

rest_days.note: Essentially equal, ~5 hour edge to Norway -- not a meaningful factor.

games.note: DNP -- did not feature, no minutes recorded

haaland_tournament_log.summary: Scored in all 4 games he appeared in (7 goals total), 1 DNP (vs France, group stage -- reason not confirmed, possibly rotation). Tournament's outright leading scorer. StatsBomb historical panel: 0 rows (Norway has not appeared in a men's WC since 1998, so Haaland has no prior major-tournament data at any age).

kane_tournament_log.summary: 2+ SOT in 3/5 games (0.60). StatsBomb historical (2018+2022, n=12): 2+ SOT in only 2/12 games (0.167) -- large historical/current-form gap, see PRICING_METHODOLOGY.md.

bellingham_tournament_log.summary: 2+ SOT in 3/5 games (0.60), but driven almost entirely by the last 2 games (3, 3). StatsBomb historical (2022 WC only, n=5, too young for 2018): 2+ SOT in 0/5 games -- his 2022 ceiling was 1 SOT. Sharp divergence between recent 2026 form and full historical record -- see PRICING_METHODOLOGY.md, this triggered a mandatory recheck under the restored RULE1 discipline.

norway.note: Norway has 0 StatsBomb historical rows (last men's WC appearance 1998) -- team-level count-prop priors shrink toward the 2026 tournament-wide corpus mean instead of a team-specific historical mean, a genuine methodology fork from every prior match in this project. Norway scored AND conceded in literally every one of its 5 games (5/5 BTTS).

england.note: R16 vs Mexico was a chaotic outlier: only 6 total shots but 4 yellows + 1 red -- lowest-volume, highest-discipline-cost game of the tournament for England. BTTS in 3/5 games.

corpus_2026_wide_priors.note: Used as Norway's shrinkage prior in place of a team-specific historical mean (see above).

sub_scorer_base_rate.method: scanned every locally saved full ESPN match dump with usable roster stats (n=60 unique matches); a match counts as a hit if any player with subIns>=1 also has goals>0 or assists>0 in that match

second_half_more_goals_base_rate.method: parsed keyEvents goal timestamps by period across the same 60-match corpus

card_in_each_half_base_rate.method: parsed keyEvents card timestamps by period across the same 60-match corpus

referee.name: Clement Turpin
referee.source: web search (Tribuna.com, nysportsday.com, law5-theref.blogspot.com), corroborated against our own ESPN data
referee.note_on_source: ESPN gameInfo.officials for event 760512 returns null (doesn't publish future assignments) -- identity from external reporting, corroborated by independently pulling his 2 other confirmed WC2026 matches directly from ESPN.
referee.sample_caveat: n=2, thinner than the Oliver sample (n=3) used for Spain vs Belgium -- treat as a weak signal, weighted accordingly in the blend (see 03_model_derivations.json).
